name: Joe Louis
abstract: Joseph Louis Barrow (May 13, 1914 – April 12, 1981) was an American professional boxer who competed from 1934 to 1951. Nicknamed "the Brown Bomber", Louis is widely regarded as one of the greatest and most influential boxers of all time.

concerts.label: All-Star Benefit Concert: Booker T. Washington Memorial Fund

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: I arrived at that title from Joe Louis, that did this shuffling, you know, with his feet in prize-fighting.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Holloway
about_quotes.evidence: I always liked boxing because, you know like Sugar Ray and Joe Louis they were my idols, you know?
about_quotes.doc_title: Red Holloway interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: But he looked like Joe Louis. That size, build, face, features, everything.

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: Ken(?) Magazine had a headline, 'Nazis Try To Kidnap Joe Louis.'

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Louis happened to be in the city and was staying in the same hotel. We had an interview with one of the leading Negro newspapers and made some photos with Joe. I was shaking his hand on one of them

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmie Crawford
about_quotes.evidence: I knew Joe Louis well, Ray Robinson well, and they were my two friendly pals.
